issues: Authorization logic in addGuests.handler.ts:51-52 uses && instead of ||, requiring users to be both admin AND owner. This means team admins who aren't also owners are silently denied access.
Passing 0 as a fallback teamId when there's no team causes a query for a non-existent team instead of short-circuiting the authorization check.

Duplicate Zod schema: AddGuestsDialog.tsx:29-33 defines a ZAddGuestsInputSchema that already exists in addGuests.schema.ts. The client-side version adds a uniqueness refinement the server lacks, and the server version wraps it in an object the client doesn't. There's no single source of truth for validation.
Guest attendees are assigned the organizer's timezone and locale in addGuests.handler.ts:87-92, so a guest in a different timezone receives calendar invites with incorrect time information.

Redundant database query: the initial booking query at addGuests.handler.ts:24-45 already includes the user with credentials, but a separate organizer query at lines 57-66 fetches a subset of the same user fields, causing an unnecessary database round-trip.

Silent email failure in addGuests.handler.ts:166-168: the catch block discards the actual error object and only logs a generic string via console.log. The user gets a success response, guests are never notified, and there's no debugging information.

BLACKLISTED_GUEST_EMAILS env var is parsed on every request with no caching or validation. Guest emails aren't lowercased before comparison, so mixed-case emails like User@Example.com bypass the blacklist.

The MultiEmail component may already exist in the codebase (packages/ui/form/MultiEmail.tsx). The lazy loading wrapper comment suggests it was copied from somewhere rather than reusing an existing multi-email input component.

No shared event-building logic: the ~50 line CalendarEvent construction block in addGuests.handler.ts:103-149 is hand-assembled with the same pattern as editLocation.handler, and will drift between handlers over time.
multiEmailValue is initialized as [""] rather than [] in AddGuestsDialog.tsx:35, creating a phantom empty string in the array and requiring a workaround length === 0 check in handleAdd.
Inconsistent naming: add_members is used as the action id but additional_guests as the label in BookingListItem.tsx:193-199.
The isInvalidEmail state in AddGuestsDialog is set to true but never cleared on subsequent valid input, so the error persists until the user closes and reopens the dialog.
